channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Early 20th Century Proposal to Deploy Canadian Troops to India Under British Rule
item.description: At the turn of the 20th century, a proposal emerged within the British Empire to deploy Canadian troops to India for training and frontier defence. The scheme, known as Sir Frederick Borden’s Scheme, ...

item.title: Mike Pezzullo advocates for increased defence readiness despite past controversies
item.description: Michael Pezzullo, former head of the Department of Home Affairs, has publicly expressed a strong desire for military engagement and increased defence preparedness. The article portrays Pezzullo as a c...

item.title: Restoring boreal forests in northern Europe to support reindeer herding and Sámi livelihoods
item.description: Political debates in Sweden and the European Union regarding forest management have reached a deadlock between increasing wood production and prioritising nature conservation and carbon storage. Howev...

item.title: Athena Retains Ring of Honor Women’s Championship, Aims for Historic Reign
item.description: Athena continued her dominant run in professional wrestling by retaining the Ring of Honor Women’s World Championship at the Supercard of Honor event. The championship was contested in a challenging S...

item.title: Martha Karua Criticises Rising Fuel Prices Under Ruto, Draws Parallels to Moi Era
item.description: Martha Karua, the leader of the People’s Liberation Party (PLP), has drawn sharp comparisons between President William Ruto’s administration and the government of former President Daniel Arap Moi, fol...

item.title: Cayetano’s Use of Faith in Politics Raises Questions on Accountability
item.description: The article critically examines the public persona and political career of Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano, highlighting the apparent contradictions between his professed Christian faith and his ...

item.title: PCOS Now Called PMOS to Reflect Hormonal and Metabolic Health in Women
item.description: For long, many women wey dey suffer from PCOS dey confuse because di name no really represent wetin dey happen for dia body. As of May 2026, di medical world don officially rename di condition to PMOS...

item.title: US Business Leaders Engage Chinese Officials to Boost Trade and Investments
item.description: Several top US CEOs from aviation, technology, finance, and commodities sectors visited Beijing alongside former President Donald Trump to strengthen business ties with China. Companies like GE Aerosp...

item.title: Ooni Of Ife Shares Experience With Public Pressure Before Birth Of Twins
item.description: The Ooni of Ife, Oba Adeyeye Ogunwusi, don talk true about the kind wahala and mockery wey e face over the delay to get children after e become Ooni. For statement wey e director of media, Moses Olafa...

item.title: The History and Politics Behind Kenya's Campaign Against Traditional Alcohol
item.description: Back in the early 1900s, home-made alcohol was part of everyday life in Kenya, especially among communities like the Mijikenda, where palm wine played a key role in ceremonies and rituals. The colonia...

item.title: Russian President Putin to hold strategic talks in China with Xi Jinping
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is scheduled to visit China on 19th May for a two-day official trip, immediately following US President Donald Trump's recent visit to Beijing. The Kremlin announced t...

item.title: Colorado Governor Grants Clemency to Tina Peters After Controversial Sentence
item.description: Tina Peters, a 70-year-old former Mesa County clerk, had been serving a nine-year sentence for nonviolent actions related to her attempts to examine vulnerabilities in Colorado's electronic voting sys...

item.title: US and Nigerian forces successfully eliminate ISIS deputy leader Abu-Bilal al-Minuki
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced on May 15, 2026, that Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the second-in-command of ISIS worldwide, was killed in a joint operation conducted by American and Nigerian forces. Al-Mi...

item.title: Alberta Wildfire Prompts Evacuations in Clearwater County Backcountry
item.description: A rapidly spreading wildfire in Clearwater County, northwest of Sundre, Alberta, has prompted evacuation alerts for backcountry users and campers. The fire, detected Thursday and fanned by strong wind...

item.title: Best Victoria Day 2026 Sales on Home, Fashion, and Beauty Items
item.description: This Victoria Day long weekend, shoppers in Canada can take advantage of significant discounts across a wide range of products. The article highlights top deals on home furniture, kitchen appliances, ...

item.title: Deon Fourie to miss several months with knee injury ahead of URC play-offs
item.description: Stormers stand-in captain Deon Fourie will be sidelined for several months following a medial ligament knee injury sustained during the URC match against Ulster in Belfast. The 39-year-old veteran was...

item.title: Venezuela Fury, 16, prepares to marry Noah Price, 19, at Isle of Man church with family and Netflix crew present
item.description: Venezuela Fury, the 16-year-old daughter of boxer Tyson Fury and his wife Paris, is set to marry her 19-year-old fiancé Noah Price at the Victorian Royal Chapel of St John's on the Isle of Man. Weddin...

item.title: Reese Witherspoon’s Breakthrough Role in 'Freeway' at Age 19
item.description: In 1995, a 19-year-old Reese Witherspoon took on the challenging role of Vanessa Lutz in the dark, edgy film 'Freeway.' Director Matthew Bright described her performance as electrifying, comparing it ...

item.title: Daily Horoscope Guidance for May 16, 2026
item.description: This horoscope article provides insights and guidance for each zodiac sign for May 16, 2026. It highlights a birthday new moon, suggesting it is a period of potential success, particularly if ambition...

item.title: Abrahams Clarifies No Political Interference in TRC Cases Despite Resource Challenges
